coverage_notes: No live MCP calls attempted (connectors flagged as failing; staged data + repo were sufficient for the a11y scope). Contrast audit computed for every text/background token pair actually used in app/globals.css :root — PASSES verified, not just failures: --text #0A0A0A on --surface 19.80:1; --text-2 #2A2A2A 14.35:1; --text-3 #6B6B6B on --surface 5.33:1 and on --surface-2 4.97:1 (both pass AA); --text-on-inv #FFF on --surface-inv #1B2C5E 13.39:1; --text-on-inv-2 #B5B5B5 on navy 6.53:1 (pass); --accent #1428A0 on white 11.41:1; white on --accent 11.41:1; white on --accent-2 14.05:1; --sale #D8232A on white 5.01:1 (pass); --trust #1A7F4E on white 5.01:1 (pass); topbar rgba(255,255,255,.78/.62) blended over navy = 8.77/6.11:1 (pass); .announcement-bar-urgent white on #b91c1c 6.47:1 (pass); .pdp-save #D8232A on 8% red tint ~4.8:1 (pass at 12px/600). Borderline-but-passing: --text-3 on --surface-3 is 4.23:1, but no actual text-on-surface-3 pairing exists in globals.css (surface-3 is only progress-track fills and .round-btn:hover whose icon is --text at 19:1), so not reported. --brand-red on navy computes 2.67:1 but no text pairing of red-on-navy was found in CSS (only decorative .brand-stripe and icons on white), so not reported. Known-strong items re-verified silently: skip link + :focus-visible ring (globals.css:140-155), announcer live regions (plp-toolbar-count aria-live=polite, FilterPanel announce()), focus trap + Esc close in filter-shell.tsx:24, icon-only buttons all carry aria-label in all 6 captures (0 unnamed buttons found by parser), FilterMobileTrigger aria-label includes active count. False positives excluded after raw-markup verification: 33-44 'unlabeled' PLP checkboxes are implicitly wrapped in

findings.title: Unavailable variant chips render 13-14px text at 2.05:1 — fails WCAG 1.4.3 and even the 3:1 large-text floor, and the chips are NOT disabled controls
findings.evidence: app/globals.css:2983 `.pdp-size.unavailable { color: var(--text-4); ... }` and :3005 `.pdp-firm.unavailable { color: var(--text-4); ... }` with --text-4 = #B5B5B5 (globals.css:23). Computed: #B5B5B5 on --surface #FFFFFF = 2.05:1; on the `.on` state's --surface-2 #F7F7F7 = 1.91:1. The chips remain interactive <button>s (no disabled attr) — app/(storefront)/products/[handle]/buy-box.tsx:229-236 renders `className={...available ? '' : 'unavailable'}` with an active onClick and aria-label="$v (unavailable)"; same pattern in sticky-variant-sheet.tsx:124. Because they are operable, the WCAG 1.4.3 'inactive component' exemption does not apply. Labels are .pdp-size-label 13px/600 and .pdp-firm-label 14px/600 — not large text.
findings.impact: Low-vision shoppers on any PDP with out-of-stock sizes/firmnesses cannot read which options exist but are unavailable — the exact options a shopper most needs to compare before calling a showroom or picking another size. Affects both the desktop buy box and the mobile sticky variant sheet across all 218 products.
findings.proposed_fix: Change `.pdp-size.unavailable` / `.pdp-firm.unavailable` text color from var(--text-4) to var(--text-3) #6B6B6B (5.33:1 on white, 4.97:1 on surface-2 — passes AA) and keep the diagonal strikethrough ::after as the second, non-color cue. If a lighter look is wanted, #767676 (4.54:1) is the lightest AA-passing gray on white. Leave `.pdp-stepper button:disabled` (globals.css:3035) as-is — it carries a real disabled attribute and is exempt.

findings.title: Footer nav landmarks 'Customer Care' and 'Visit Us' have broken accessible names: aria-labelledby IDREF contains a space
findings.evidence: app/_components/footer.tsx:100 `const navId = `footer-col-${c.title.toLowerCase()}`;` produces id="footer-col-customer care" and id="footer-col-visit us" — confirmed in the live capture homepage.html: `<nav aria-labelledby="footer-col-customer care">` and `<h3 id="footer-col-customer care" class="eyebrow">Customer Care`. aria-labelledby is a space-separated ID reference list, so browsers tokenize the value into two lookups ('footer-col-customer' + 'care'), neither of which resolves; the space also makes the id itself invalid for IDREF use. Present on every page (footer is global) in all 5 content captures.
findings.impact: Two of the four footer nav landmarks expose no name in screen-reader landmark lists (users hear 'navigation' x2 alongside named 'Shop'/'About'), defeating the exact landmark-navigation intent documented in the code comment at footer.tsx:102-106. Small but global, and a 1-line fix.
findings.proposed_fix: Slugify the title when building the id: `const navId = `footer-col-${c.title.toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, '-')}`;` → footer-col-customer-care / footer-col-visit-us.

findings.title: Live 404 responses serve an empty __next_error__ shell: no lang attribute, no headings, no landmarks, no links in the server HTML
findings.evidence: Capture 404-page.html (45,749 bytes): document starts `...` — the element has NO lang attribute (all 5 other captures have ``), and `
findings.impact: Screen-reader and no-JS/failed-JS users hitting any dead URL get a literally blank, language-less document (WCAG 3.1.1 fail on the served page; nothing to read until hydration completes). Also overlaps SEO/perf: bots that don't execute JS see an empty 404 body. Every typo URL, discontinued-product URL, and stale backlink hits this.
findings.proposed_fix: Find the CSR bailout that prevents the not-found boundary from server-rendering (typical culprits: useSearchParams/usePathname without a Suspense boundary in the root layout chain, or notFound() thrown after streaming starts in (storefront)/pages/[handle]). Verify with `next build` output that /_not-found prerenders, and confirm a curl of a dead URL returns HTML containing `<html lang="en">` and the 'We couldn't find that page.' h1.

findings.title: Product-rail scroll buttons force smooth scrolling regardless of prefers-reduced-motion
findings.evidence: app/_components/sections/rail-scroll-buttons.tsx:28 `el?.scrollBy({ left: dir * step, behavior: 'smooth' });` — unconditional. The CSS reduced-motion kill switch (app/globals.css:289-291 `* { transition: none !important; animation: none !important; }`) cannot affect JS-initiated scrollBy animation. Contrast with hero-controller.tsx:54, which correctly checks `window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)')` before autoplaying, and admin smooth scroll which is gated behind `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` at globals.css:6361.
findings.impact: Vestibular-sensitive users who set reduced motion still get a ~400px animated horizontal glide every time they page a product rail (homepage 'Pairs well with', PLP rails). Isolated, but it is the only motion on the site that escapes the otherwise-complete reduced-motion system.
findings.proposed_fix: const reduce = window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches; el?.scrollBy({ left: dir * step, behavior: reduce ? 'auto' : 'smooth' });

findings.title: Blog archive skips from h1 straight to h3 for all 12 featured/recent article cards
findings.evidence: blog-archive-beds-mattresses.html heading sequence: h1 'LA Mattress stores blog' → h3 'Mattress Stores Los Angeles: Your 2026 Shopping Guide' (+11 more h3 cards) with no intervening h2; the first h2 ('All LA Mattress stores blog articles') only appears AFTER the card grid. Parser output: SKIPS: [((1, 'LA Mattress stores blog'), (3, 'Mattress Stores Los Angeles: ...'))], sequence [1,3,3,3,3,3,3,3,3,3,3,3,3,2,2,...].
findings.impact: Screen-reader users skimming by heading level (NVDA 2-key) hear an empty h2 level above the card list and may assume the featured articles are subordinate to a section they missed. Cosmetic-to-moderate; single template.
findings.proposed_fix: Either demote nothing and add a visually-hidden h2 (e.g. 'Latest articles') before the card grid in the blog index component (the .gd-* blog index template), or promote the card titles to h2 to sit directly under the h1.

findings.title: Desktop primary nav is the only unlabeled nav landmark among 6-8 on every page
findings.evidence: app/_components/nav.tsx:367 `<nav className="nav-items">` — no aria-label. Live PLP capture contains 8 nav elements; the other 7 are all named: aria-label="Breadcrumb", "Shop related collections", "Store benefits", 4x footer aria-labelledby, and the mobile counterpart at nav.tsx:544 is `<nav className="mobile-list" aria-label="Primary">`. Mega submenu navs are also labeled (nav.tsx:479).
findings.impact: In screen-reader landmark lists the most important nav on the page is the one announced as bare 'navigation' among 6-8 landmarks, making it harder to jump to. Trivial fix, global benefit.
findings.proposed_fix: nav.tsx:367 → `<nav className="nav-items" aria-label="Primary">` (matches the mobile drawer's existing label so desktop/mobile announce identically).

findings.title: Two different blog articles share the identical title/link text 'How to design a bedroom that actually helps you sleep'
findings.evidence: blog-archive-beds-mattresses.html: identical link text resolves to two distinct targets — /blogs/beds-mattresses/bedroom-therapy-desann-collins and /blogs/beds-mattresses/bedroom-therapy-heather-vieira. This was the only identical-text/different-target collision across all 6 captures (all 551 links checked).
findings.impact: WCAG 2.4.4: link purpose is indistinguishable for SR users tabbing a links list — they cannot tell the Desann Collins interview from the Heather Vieira one. Content-level, 2 URLs.
findings.proposed_fix: Differentiate the article titles in Shopify (e.g. append the interviewee: '... — with Desann Collins' / '... — with Heather Vieira'); no code change needed.
